subagent_reports.topic: Validate criteria-ownership model (firm behavioral AC before design, design-derived technical criteria after) against established RE/agile/stage-gate practice

sources.label: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148:2011/2018 (RE standard) — Implementation-Free / what-not-how
sources.summary: Canonical good-requirement characteristic 'Implementation Free': requirement states WHAT is required not HOW; avoids placing unnecessary constraints on architectural design; objective is implementation-independent. Directly endorses design-independent behavioral AC. Also: requirements must be verifiable/testable and atomic. Crucially includes the re-entry caveat (see below).

sources.label: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148 — iterative/recursive RE+architecture caveat (re-entry control)
sources.summary: 'as requirements are allocated and decomposed through the levels of the system, there can be recognition of design decisions/solution architectures defined at a higher level. This is part of the iterative and recursive application of the requirements, architecture and design processes.' = standards-level endorsement of design-derived technical criteria AND of design feeding back to refine requirements (the re-entry valve).

sources.label: Marty Cagan / SVPG — Dual-Track Agile
sources.summary: Discovery track produces VALIDATED backlog items; validation happens DURING discovery, before production code (vs waterfall validating after release). Confirms firming criteria forward of delivery is the named, established model.

sources.label: Dual-track agile Definition of Ready handoff (evidence-anchored AC before delivery)
sources.summary: Items move Discovery→Delivery on a Definition of Ready: validated problem, acceptance criteria, telemetry/test strategy written. Named failure mode: 'ideas flood delivery without clear acceptance criteria, causing churn and rework' — i.e. firming AC before build is the explicit control against churn.

sources.label: PM StackExchange — keep AC independent of solution/UI
sources.summary: 'Keep your acceptance criteria independent of the solution... AC capture the basic requirements without being tied too closely to the UI or solution design.' BUT cautions against forcing a strictly LINEAR flow: design activity may legitimately surface new/changed AC → iterate. Direct support for both the design-independence rule and the re-entry valve; warning against rigid one-way linearity.

sources.label: Scrum.org / Mountain Goat (Cohn) — DoD vs Acceptance Criteria
sources.summary: AC = per-item, user-perspective, what success looks like (extrinsic quality). DoD = universal engineering/quality bar (intrinsic quality). Maps cleanly: PROPOSAL user-outcomes + DISCOVERY behavioral AC = extrinsic/AC family; DESIGN-derived technical criteria lean toward intrinsic/DoD family. The two-axis split is a recognized, named distinction.

sources.label: Gojko Adzic — Specification by Example; LeSS A-TDD; Fowler
sources.summary: Collaborative requirements discovery: examples/executable acceptance tests specified WITH user/stakeholders in workshops BEFORE/concurrent with development; 'conversations leading to examples often more valuable than the tests.' Validates discovery-owned, user-confirmed, testable behavioral AC. Fowler caveat: SbE 'can't be the only requirements technique' — examples need generalization.

subagent_reports.architecture_assessment: The criteria-ownership model is strongly aligned with canonical practice; it is essentially a named recombination of three established ideas, not a novel/clever invention. (1) DISCOVERY owning design-INDEPENDENT behavioral AC = ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148 'Implementation-Free' requirement characteristic (state what, not how; implementation-independent; verifiable; atomic) + dual-track agile's 'validated backlog item / Definition of Ready' handoff. (2) DESIGN owning design-DERIVED technical criteria/budgets = 29148's explicit recursive caveat that design decisions/solution architectures are recognized as requirements are decomposed through system levels — i.e. derived constraints are expected downstream, not upstream. This design-independent-vs-design-derived split is real and named in practice as stakeholder/system requirements (extrinsic, what) vs derived/design constraints + intrinsic quality (how), and as AC vs DoD (extrinsic vs intrinsic quality). (3) The 'design → discovery re-entry when design invalidates a user-facing AC' valve is itself a standards-endorsed control: 29148 frames RE+architecture as iterative/recursive, and practitioners explicitly warn against a strictly linear flow — design work legitimately surfaces new/changed AC and must loop back (change control / iterative refinement / requirements churn management). PROPOSAL owning non-gating user-outcomes vs DISCOVERY owning testable AC mirrors the well-established business-goal/outcome vs conditions-of-satisfaction layering (Cohn, Scrum.org) and is NOT a false distinction. Forward ordering (firm behavioral AC before design, with escape valve) is exactly dual-track agile's posture: validate before build to avoid churn/rework, but never enforce one-way linearity.

validation.notes: Model passes LBP validation: it is boring/proven, not clever. The ONLY caution is enforcement RIGIDITY, not the ownership split itself. Mature methods uniformly warn that a strictly linear AC-then-design flow is an anti-pattern; the re-entry valve is what makes the model correct. Spec deltas MUST make re-entry cheap and expected, not exceptional. Two guardrails the deltas should encode explicitly: (a) Some criteria genuinely CANNOT be firmed without architectural knowledge (performance/security/scale budgets) — these are correctly DESIGN-derived; do NOT force them into discovery. Discovery AC must stay black-box/observable per 29148. (b) Guard against the inverse failure: discovery AC secretly encoding design ('AC that smell like solutions' — 29148 implementation-free check + 'keep AC independent of solution'). Recommend a discovery-exit lint: any AC referencing a mechanism/component/architecture is mis-placed and belongs in design.

subagent_reports.recommendation: Ship the model. Adopt established terminology to anchor it and reduce reinvention friction: (1) Use 29148 'Implementation-Free' as the test for discovery AC (what-not-how, black-box/observable, atomic, verifiable). (2) Call DISCOVERY criteria 'behavioral / black-box acceptance criteria' and DESIGN criteria 'derived requirements / design constraints' (29148 vocabulary) or frame as extrinsic (AC, user-perceived) vs intrinsic (DoD/engineering) quality (Scrum.org/Cohn). (3) Name the discovery→delivery handoff a 'Definition of Ready' (dual-track agile) and the design→discovery loop 'iterative/recursive refinement' or 'requirements re-validation' (29148) — frame it as normal change control, NOT failure. (4) Add a discovery-exit guard rejecting AC that encode a chosen mechanism, and an explicit category for design-derived budgets so teams don't try to firm un-firmable criteria in discovery. (5) Optionally lean on Specification-by-Example / Gherkin for the 'firmed and confirmed with the user' step — established mechanism for user-confirmed testable AC — but heed Fowler's caveat that examples are not the sole requirements technique.
follow_ups: Spec deltas should make design→discovery re-entry low-friction and explicitly 'expected', not an exception path — biggest risk is the model hardening into rigid linear waterfall.
Add a discovery-gate guard: reject/flag AC that reference a specific mechanism/component/architecture (29148 implementation-free violation); these belong in design.
Add an explicit 'design-derived budget/constraint' criteria category so performance/security/scale limits that cannot be firmed pre-architecture are not forced into discovery.
Consider naming alignment with 29148 (Implementation-Free, derived requirements) and dual-track (Definition of Ready) so the model reads as established practice rather than bespoke ADV invention.

Did not separately verify Volere/BABOK/Cockburn/Cooper stage-gate primary sources this pass (bounded research); 29148 + dual-track + SbE + Scrum DoD/AC corpus already give converging multi-source support. State explicitly if deeper stage-gate-specific citation is later required.

subagent_reports.topic: Design validation: sharpen workflow stage boundaries (retire checkMissingSuccessCriteria, rename proposal Success Criteria -> User Outcomes)

sources.label: clarify-readiness.ts checkMissingSuccessCriteria + runClarifyReadinessChecks
sources.summary: Reads proposal '## Success Criteria' (line 137-138); emits CLARIFY_MISSING_SUCCESS_CRITERIA as severity 'warning' only. Confirms retire surface and heading-regex collision.

sources.label: gate.ts planning-gate clarify invocation
sources.summary: clarify_enforcement defaults to 'advisory'; findings only HARD-BLOCK under 'strict' mode. So checkMissingSuccessCriteria only hard-blocks in strict mode today; advisory/off it is a warning.

sources.label: contract-mint.ts buildContractFromAgreement
sources.summary: Parses input.agreement (NOT proposal). CONTRACT_ITEMS_EMPTY requires >=1 of SC/AC/C/DONT/OOS of ANY kind, not AC/SC specifically. Confirms rename claim 3b and reveals contract mint does not require an acceptance criterion specifically.

sources.label: gate-readiness.ts discovery enforcement chain
sources.summary: discovery gate is artifact-backed on 'agreement' (L2 stateBackedArtifactEvidence -> ARTIFACT_MISSING if no agreement). discoveryContractBlockers fires DISCOVERY_CONTRACT_MISSING when agreementExists && !contract. Combined: discovery cannot complete without an agreement AND a minted contract.

sources.label: context-snapshot.countSuccessCriteria + chat-output-display spec
sources.summary: countSuccessCriteria parses proposal '## Success Criteria'; feeds the chat-output-display MUST requirement 'success criteria count'. Design step 3 acknowledges repoint/drop.

sources.label: adv-proposal spec success-criteria dependencies
sources.summary: Multiple MUST requirements mandate proposal 'success criteria' (rq-prop-out1, rq-prop-scope1/1.2, rq-prop-tax2.1). Design Key Decision #5 names exactly these lines for edit.

sources.label: CLARIFY_MISSING_SUCCESS_CRITERIA live references
sources.summary: Live code references bounded to those files; 50+ archive change.json hits are frozen historical records, not type-checked consumers. Enum is string-keyed object, removal is type-safe.

sources.label: json.ts proposal scaffolds emit '## Success Criteria'
sources.summary: Two scaffold templates WRITE '## Success Criteria' into new proposal.md. They emit (not parse) the heading; design did not explicitly enumerate them.

subagent_reports.architecture_assessment: The load-bearing claim (decision #1: retire-not-repoint preserves net criteria enforcement) is SOUND for the enforcement-gap question. The discovery gate is doubly gated: (a) artifact-backed on agreement.md (stateBackedArtifactEvidence -> ARTIFACT_MISSING/UNDERSIZED if absent or <20 non-whitespace chars), and (b) DISCOVERY_CONTRACT_MISSING once an agreement exists and no contract is minted. Contract mint requires >=1 contract item (CONTRACT_ITEMS_EMPTY). Therefore no change can reach planning/execution without an agreement + a minted contract. Removing checkMissingSuccessCriteria removes only (i) a severity:'warning' advisory in advisory/off modes and (ii) a strict-mode hard block on the PROPOSAL artifact. The post-design enforcement floor (agreement + contract present) is structurally stronger than a proposal-text regex. IMPORTANT NUANCE (not an enforcement-gap regression, but a precision caveat): neither contract mint nor the acceptance gate require an item of kind 'acceptance_criterion' SPECIFICALLY -- CONTRACT_ITEMS_EMPTY accepts any of SC/AC/C/DONT/OOS, and acceptanceContractBlockers iterate all verificationRequired items regardless of kind. This is unchanged by this design (it predates it) and AC8 preserves it, so it is not introduced here; but the design's framing 'discovery contract mint already blocks when AC/SC are absent' is slightly imprecise -- it blocks when ALL contract items are absent, not specifically when AC/SC are absent. Net: no NEW gap created; the pre-existing 'any-item-suffices' behavior is retained per AC8. Rename claim (3b) verified: contract-mint SECTION_MAPPINGS parses agreement.md only, so the proposal rename causes no acceptance-gate regression. The only proposal-text PARSERS of '## Success Criteria' are clarify-readiness + context-snapshot (both in scope); json.ts/change.ts templates are WRITERS that should follow the rename for consistency.

validation.notes: VERDICT: VALIDATED with CAUTIONs (no conflicts). Dim1 CORRECTNESS=pass: no enforcement gap; discovery agreement+contract gating is the structural floor. Dim2 SIMPLICITY=pass: retire-not-repoint is correct and simpler; no path reaches planning without contract; legacy/in-flight handled by AC4-avoidance (no shim) since the removed check was warning/strict-only, not load-bearing. Dim3 SPEC-LAW=pass-with-caution: removal is spec-safe, but adv-proposal has 6 MUST requirement sites mandating proposal 'success criteria' that MUST all be edited (design Key Decision #5 + grep-sweep mitigation covers this) and chat-output-display 'success criteria count' MUST requirement depends on countSuccessCriteria (design step 3 covers repoint/drop). Dim3b=pass: contract-mint reads agreement.md. Dim4 ALTERNATIVES=pass: rejected re-point alternative correctly reasoned; design also retains sibling clarify checks. CAUTIONS to carry into prep: (C-a) precision wording -- contract enforces 'any item present', not 'AC/SC present'; align spec/command text accordingly. (C-b) enumerate json.ts:591/661 + change.ts:166-172 proposal scaffold templates in the edit list so new proposals emit '## User Outcomes' not '## Success Criteria' (else scaffolded proposals mismatch the renamed model and any User-Outcomes presence check). (C-c) confirm tool-formatters.ts:269 entry removal and that types/changes.ts:171 comment example is updated.

subagent_reports.recommendation: Proceed to design-gate completion. The design is architecturally sound and the load-bearing enforcement-gap claim holds: the discovery gate's agreement-artifact L2 check plus DISCOVERY_CONTRACT_MISSING contract blocker form a structural enforcement floor that supersedes the retired proposal-text warning, so removing checkMissingSuccessCriteria creates no path to planning/execution without an agreement+contract. Three non-blocking cautions should be folded into prep tasks: (1) tighten spec/command wording -- the contract enforces 'at least one contract item', not 'AC/SC specifically' (the design's framing slightly overstates the existing guarantee, though AC8 correctly preserves current behavior); (2) add json.ts:591/661 and change.ts:166-172 proposal scaffold templates to the rename edit list so freshly scaffolded proposals emit '## User Outcomes'; (3) verify the complete bounded removal set for CLARIFY_MISSING_SUCCESS_CRITERIA (clarify-readiness.ts def+2 uses, clarify-readiness.test.ts, tool-formatters.ts:269, types/changes.ts:171 comment). None of these change the verdict; the retire-not-repoint decision is correct and simpler than the rejected alternative.
follow_ups: Prep: edit all 6 adv-proposal success-criteria MUST sites (spec.json:5,12,23,43,60,152) via grep-sweep gate.
Prep: enumerate json.ts:591/661 + change.ts:166-172 scaffold templates in rename scope.
Prep: tighten spec/command wording on contract enforcement granularity (any-item vs AC/SC-specific) to match actual CONTRACT_ITEMS_EMPTY semantics.
Prep: confirm context-snapshot countSuccessCriteria repoint/drop keeps chat-output-display 'success criteria count' MUST satisfied.

items.text: Each pre-execution stage owns one distinct concern with no upstream leakage of AC-grade or design work.
The proposal stage is materially lighter — no testable-success-criteria obligation, no design decisions.
The criteria-ownership model reads as established practice (29148 / dual-track), not bespoke ADV invention.
No regression to the acceptance-gate contract machinery; full build green.

Given a new proposal run, when it completes the proposal gate, then it is not required to produce testable/INVEST success criteria or engineering AC, and a proposal lacking testable success criteria produces no blocking or planning-gate finding on that basis.

Given the proposal spec + command, when inspected, then proposal owns a `## User Outcomes` section (implementation-free, non-gating) and explicitly defers engineering AC + success criteria to discovery.

Given a discovery run, when agreement is formed, then discovery firms and user-confirms design-independent behavioral acceptance criteria + success criteria, and these mint into the ChangeContract (`AC*`/`SC*`) as today.

Given a discovery AC that encodes a mechanism or component, when discovery runs its criteria scan, then ADV emits an advisory finding marking it preliminary/likely design-derived, without hard-blocking the discovery gate.

Given the design spec + command, when inspected, then design owns design-derived technical criteria and is prohibited from inventing new user-facing AC, and the design→discovery re-entry path for invalidated AC is documented as routine.

Given a change with no proposal-level success-criteria section, when it reaches the planning gate, then no clarify-readiness finding fires on proposal success-criteria, and criteria enforcement is anchored to discovery's agreement/contract.
Given the full change, when `pnpm run check` and the test suite run, then both pass, including updated asset tests for the four command files and the affected validator, snapshot, and manifest tests.
Given the acceptance gate, when a change is reviewed, then the ChangeContract `AC*` review matrix and evidence policies behave exactly as before this change.
Specs-as-laws: all spec deltas authored, validated, and conformant before release.
Preserve the crash-safe, artifact-backed gate model.
Honor `clarify_enforcement` feature-flag semantics (off / advisory / strict).
No regression to ChangeContract `AC*` minting, review matrix, or evidence policies.
Do not conflate proposal `User Outcomes` with engineering acceptance criteria.
Do not hard-block the discovery gate on the implementation-free guard (advisory only).
Do not redesign the `/adv-task` fast-track in this change (tracked follow-up `ag-JpHqmo1c`).
Do not add backward-compatibility migration shims for existing proposals (no special handling).
Do not silently change the `clarify_enforcement` default.
Execution, acceptance, and release gate responsibilities.
ChangeContract `AC*` schema or evidence-policy redesign.
`/adv-task` fast-track redesign (follow-up `ag-JpHqmo1c`).
Backward-compat migration for in-flight changes carrying proposal success criteria.

acceptanceCriteria: Given a new proposal run, when it completes the proposal gate, then it is not required to produce testable/INVEST success criteria or engineering AC, and a proposal lacking testable success criteria produces no blocking or planning-gate finding on that basis.

Given the proposal spec + command, when inspected, then proposal owns a `## User Outcomes` section (implementation-free, non-gating) and explicitly defers engineering AC + success criteria to discovery.

Given a discovery run, when agreement is formed, then discovery firms and user-confirms design-independent behavioral acceptance criteria + success criteria, and these mint into the ChangeContract (`AC*`/`SC*`) as today.

Given a discovery AC that encodes a mechanism or component, when discovery runs its criteria scan, then ADV emits an advisory finding marking it preliminary/likely design-derived, without hard-blocking the discovery gate.

Given the design spec + command, when inspected, then design owns design-derived technical criteria and is prohibited from inventing new user-facing AC, and the design→discovery re-entry path for invalidated AC is documented as routine.

Given a change with no proposal-level success-criteria section, when it reaches the planning gate, then no clarify-readiness finding fires on proposal success-criteria, and criteria enforcement is anchored to discovery's agreement/contract.
Given the full change, when `pnpm run check` and the test suite run, then both pass, including updated asset tests for the four command files and the affected validator, snapshot, and manifest tests.
Given the acceptance gate, when a change is reviewed, then the ChangeContract `AC*` review matrix and evidence policies behave exactly as before this change.

documents.proposal:
## Why

ADV's four pre-execution gates leak responsibilities upstream. `adv-proposal/spec.json` mandates testable/INVEST success criteria + full scope + affected-code at proposal time, and machine enforcement (`validator/clarify-readiness.ts` `checkMissingSuccessCriteria()`, planning-gate block in `tools/gate.ts` under strict mode, `tools/status.ts` clarify nags) gives it teeth. The proposal stage over-plans and occasionally makes design decisions ahead of discovery/design. We want each pre-execution gate to own one distinct concern.

## What Changes

- **Proposal** owns: problem clarity, context gathering, user clarification, vision + direction, high-level user-perspective outcomes (user needs). Stops requiring testable success criteria, engineering AC, or design.
- **Discovery** owns: design-independent (implementation-free) behavioral acceptance criteria + success criteria — firmed and confirmed with the user — plus deep project-state and web research toward the vision.
- **Design** owns: architectural/engineering decisions and design-derived technical criteria; the plan to satisfy discovery's AC + vision. Tighten so nothing upstream pre-decides architecture.
- **Prep** owns: remaining-context gathering, technical-doc readiness for implementing agents, task mapping + sequencing.
- Rebalance the machine enforcement so proposal-level success-criteria checks no longer pull acceptance-grade work upstream; relocate/retarget that enforcement to discovery.
- Resolve a terminology collision: proposal-level criteria must NOT reuse the load-bearing engineering term. Discovery mints typed ChangeContract `AC*` items (`evidencePolicy: "test"`) that drive the acceptance-gate review matrix. Give the proposal-level artifact a distinct, non-gating name; exact name decided in this change.

## Criteria Ownership Model

A clean 3-tier split resolves the discovery-vs-design chicken-and-egg (AC that depend on the chosen architecture cannot be firmed before design):

| Stage | Criteria | Nature |
|---|---|---|
| Proposal | User outcomes | What the user needs. Value-framed, non-gating, implementation-free. |
| Discovery | Acceptance criteria (`AC*`) + success criteria (`SC*`) | Observable/behavioral contract derived from user needs + domain + spec constraints + research. Design-independent (29148 implementation-free). User-confirmable. "What must be true," not "how." |
| Design | Technical/design criteria | Budgets + constraints that fall out of the chosen architecture (perf/security/scale). Not user-facing, not user-confirmed. The plan to satisfy discovery's AC + vision. |

Invariants:

- Discovery firms only **design-independent** criteria. Design owns the HOW plus criteria the HOW forces.
- Design MUST NOT invent new user-facing AC. If a design choice can only work by changing a user-facing AC, that is contract-compromise → `adv_change_reenter` from discovery (the existing escape valve; framed as routine iterative refinement, not exceptional).
- Maps onto the existing typed contract: `AC*` (behavioral, `evidencePolicy: "test"`) + `SC*` (outcome, `review`) minted at discovery from `agreement.md`; technical criteria live in `design.md`.

## Success Criteria

- `adv-proposal` spec + command no longer require testable/INVEST success criteria; they require user-perspective outcomes instead.
- `adv-discover` spec + command explicitly own firming + confirming design-independent behavioral AC + success criteria.
- `adv-design` spec + command explicitly own design-derived technical criteria and forbid inventing new user-facing AC (re-entry path documented).
- Machine enforcement (clarify-readiness checks, planning-gate block, status nags, context-snapshot count, contract-mint heading parse) is consistent with the new placement — no check demands testable success criteria from the proposal artifact.
- Acceptance-gate machinery (ChangeContract `AC*`, review matrix, evidence policies) remains intact — no regression.
- Terminology collision resolved: proposal-level user outcomes carry a distinct, non-gating name.
- All affected specs, command files, `docs/adv-gates.md`, manifest, and tests are aligned and green: `pnpm run check` passes and the test suite is green.

## Affected Code

**Specs:** `adv-proposal`, `adv-discover`, `adv-design`, `adv-prep`, `advance-workflow`.

**Commands:** `.opencode/command/adv-proposal.md` (Phase 1b / 2 / 2.5 / 2.6), `adv-discover.md`, `adv-design.md`, `adv-prep.md`.

**Docs:** `docs/adv-gates.md` (Gate-Specific Behaviors), `ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md` (Ambiguity Taxonomy).

**Plugin code:** `validator/clarify-readiness.ts` (`checkMissingSuccessCriteria` — sole proposal-level AC-grade check), `tools/gate.ts` (planning-gate clarify block), `utils/context-snapshot.ts` (`countSuccessCriteria`), `utils/tool-formatters.ts` (`CLARIFY_MISSING_SUCCESS_CRITERIA`), `manifest.ts` (proposal description). `validator/contract-mint.ts` `SECTION_MAPPINGS` confirmed to read `agreement.md` (discovery), NOT proposal — heading collision only, no contract regression. `tools/status.ts` (ambiguity recommendations).

**Tests:** `clarify-readiness.test.ts`, `clarify-readiness.e2e.test.ts`, `status.test.ts`, `context-snapshot.test.ts`, plus per-command asset tests.

## Related Repositories

Single repo (`advance`). No cross-repo scope.

## Constraints

- Specs-as-laws: spec deltas must be authored, validated, and conformant.
- Must not regress acceptance-gate ChangeContract machinery (`AC*`, review matrix, evidence policies).
- Preserve the crash-safe, artifact-backed gate model.
- Honor `clarify_enforcement` feature-flag semantics (off / advisory / strict).
- Operate under current law until shipped (this proposal itself still carries a Success Criteria section).

## Impact

- Behavioral: lighter proposal stage; heavier, better-scoped discovery; less upstream over-planning and design leakage.
- Risk: retargeting machine enforcement could regress gate-blocking or clarify recommendations if not carefully migrated.
- Migration: in-flight changes carrying proposal-level success criteria need backward-compatible (legacy) handling.

## Context

Surfaced via `/adv-problem` triage this session. Root cause confirmed in spec law + plugin enforcement (not agent drift). User confirmed the four-stage responsibility model, the user-perspective outcomes framing for proposal, and the design-independent-vs-design-derived criteria split.

## Scope

### In Scope

- Rebalancing pre-execution stage responsibilities across the four command files + their specs + the `advance-workflow` spec.
- Retargeting/relocating machine enforcement of success-criteria from proposal to discovery.
- Resolving the proposal-level criteria terminology collision (distinct non-gating name).
- Codifying the 3-tier criteria ownership model + design→discovery re-entry path for invalidated AC.
- Aligning docs, manifest, and tests.

### Out of Scope

- Execution / acceptance / release gate responsibilities.
- ChangeContract `AC*` schema / evidence-policy redesign (must stay intact).
- Runtime tool behavior beyond what the rebalance requires.
- `/adv-task` fast-track redesign (revisit only if the rebalance forces it).

### Must Not

- Must not break the acceptance-gate review matrix or `AC*` minting.
- Must not conflate proposal-level user outcomes with testable engineering AC.
- Must not remove crash-safe artifact-backing from any gate.
- Must not silently change the `clarify_enforcement` default without an explicit decision.

## Discovery Findings

### Current State (verified)

- **ChangeContract mints from `agreement.md`, not proposal.** `contract-mint.ts` `SECTION_MAPPINGS` maps agreement headings → contract items: `Success Criteria`→`SC*`(`review`), `Acceptance Criteria`→`AC*`(`test`), `Constraints`→`C*`(`static_check`), `Avoidances`/`Do Not`→`DONT*`(`review`), `Out of Scope`→`OOS*`(`not_applicable`). Engineering criteria already form at discovery.
- **Exactly one machine check pulls AC-grade work into proposal:** `clarify-readiness.ts` `checkMissingSuccessCriteria(change, proposalText)` parses proposal `## Success Criteria`. Other proposal-level clarify checks (`checkUnclearScope`, `checkAssumptionHeavy`, `checkMissingErrorHandling`) are context/boundary checks — retain. `checkSubjectiveLanguage`/`checkMissingScenarios` read change-level fields.
- **Enforcement path:** `gate.ts` runs `runClarifyReadinessChecks` at the **planning gate**; `clarify_enforcement: "strict"` hard-blocks, `advisory` (default) warns, `status.ts` surfaces `/adv-clarify` nags. `context-snapshot.ts` `countSuccessCriteria(proposalText)` is display-only.
- **Collision is heading-level only:** because the minter and the checker both key off the literal `Success Criteria` / `Acceptance Criteria` headings, giving the proposal artifact a distinct heading avoids both — no contract regression.

### LBP Check — PASS (external validation, adv-researcher)

Model is a named recombination of established practice, not bespoke:
- Firm design-independent behavioral AC before design = ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148 "Implementation-Free" requirement characteristic.
- Forward ordering = dual-track agile; validation in discovery gated by a "Definition of Ready".
- User-outcomes (proposal) vs testable AC (discovery) mirrors business-outcome vs conditions-of-satisfaction layering (Cohn); AC vs DoD is the established two-axis split.
- Design→discovery re-entry = standards-endorsed iterative refinement.
Cautions to encode: (C1) make re-entry cheap + expected, not waterfall-rigid; (C2) don't force un-firmable budgets (perf/security/scale) into discovery — they are correctly design-derived; (C3) inverse guard — reject discovery AC that encode a mechanism/component (implementation-free violation → belongs in design). Adopt 29148/dual-track terminology.

### Conflict Scan

`adv_change_validate`: passed (NO_TASKS/NO_DELTAS expected own-change warnings). No active/archived change addresses workflow stage boundaries. `adv_agenda_list` (73 items): no overlapping item; `ag-hevnf5t8` independently notes a different change "still carries CLARIFY_MISSING_SUCCESS_CRITERIA" — corroborates the recurring friction.

### Draft Spec Deltas (shapes; final IDs in design/prep)

- `rq-stageProposalUserOutcomes01` (adv-proposal): proposal owns implementation-free user outcomes; MUST NOT require testable success criteria / engineering AC / design.
- `rq-stageDiscoveryFirmAC01` (adv-discover): discovery firms + user-confirms design-independent behavioral AC + success criteria, minted into ChangeContract from `agreement.md`.
- `rq-stageDiscoveryImplFreeGuard01` (adv-discover): discovery AC MUST be implementation-free; AC encoding a mechanism/component are flagged as belonging to design (researcher C3).
- `rq-stageDesignDerivedCriteria01` (adv-design): design owns design-derived technical criteria (perf/security/scale budgets); MUST NOT invent new user-facing AC; AC invalidated by design route back via routine `adv_change_reenter` from discovery (researcher C1/C2).
- `rq-stageEnforcementRetarget01` (advance-workflow): proposal-level success-criteria machine check no longer gates planning on the proposal artifact; SC/AC presence enforcement is anchored to discovery's agreement/contract.

### Recommended Objectives (for agreement)

1. Proposal owns problem + vision/direction + implementation-free user outcomes; drops testable success criteria / engineering AC / design.
2. Discovery owns firming + user-confirming design-independent behavioral AC + success criteria (minted into ChangeContract), with an implementation-free guard.
3. Design owns design-derived technical criteria + the plan; forbids inventing user-facing AC; routine design→discovery re-entry for invalidated AC.
4. Prep owns task mapping + technical-doc readiness; explicitly does not firm criteria.
5. Retarget machine enforcement (surgical: relocate/retire `checkMissingSuccessCriteria`; resolve heading collision); anchor SC/AC enforcement to discovery.
6. Align specs + commands + `docs/adv-gates.md` + manifest + tests; adopt 29148/dual-track terminology.
7. Backward-compatible handling for in-flight changes.

## Discovery Agenda (carry-forward / resolved)

- RESOLVED: `contract-mint.ts` reads `agreement.md` (discovery), not proposal; `SC*` consumers = acceptance review matrix.
- RESOLVED: enumerated clarify-readiness checks; only `checkMissingSuccessCriteria` is proposal-level AC-grade.
- OPEN (design): proposal-level criteria name (User Outcomes vs UAC vs other).
- OPEN (design): whether proposal user-outcomes seed discovery AC or are independent inputs.
- RESOLVED: 3-tier boundary validated against 29148/dual-track; re-entry covers design-invalidated AC.
- OPEN (this change): `clarify_enforcement` default + whether proposal retains a non-testable outcome-presence check.
- OPEN (this change): backward-compat for in-flight changes with existing proposal success criteria.
documents.problemStatement:
## Why

ADV's four pre-execution gates (proposal → discovery → design → prep) leak responsibilities upstream. The root cause is spec law plus machine enforcement, not agent drift:

- `adv-proposal/spec.json` mandates the proposal produce INVEST-quality / testable **success criteria**, full scope, and an affected-code map at proposal time (purpose line; rq lines 12, 23, 43, 60, 152).
- Machine enforcement gives it teeth: `validator/clarify-readiness.ts` `checkMissingSuccessCriteria()` parses `proposal.md` for a `## Success Criteria` section and flags missing/placeholder; `tools/gate.ts` runs those checks at the **planning gate** and, under `clarify_enforcement: "strict"`, hard-blocks; `tools/status.ts` nags via `/adv-clarify` recommendations.

Result: the proposal stage over-plans — firming acceptance-grade and solution-adjacent detail — and occasionally makes design decisions, all ahead of discovery and design where that work belongs.

Desired end-state: each pre-execution gate owns one distinct concern.

- **Proposal** — problem clarity, context gathering, user clarification, vision + direction, and high-level **user-perspective** acceptance criteria (what the user needs delivered). No engineering AC, no testable success criteria, no design.
- **Discovery** — specific context for design decisions, blocker/issue checks, deep project-state research, deep web research on the vision/direction. Engineering AC + success criteria firmed and confirmed with the user here.
- **Design** — architectural/engineering decisions proposed, web-researched, validated, affirmed; plan to deliver AC + vision.
- **Prep** — gather remaining context, ready technical docs for implementing agents, map tasks + sequence.
documents.agreement:
# Agreement

## Objectives

1. Proposal stage owns problem + vision/direction + implementation-free **User Outcomes**; drops required testable success criteria, engineering AC, and design.
2. Discovery stage owns firming + user-confirming design-independent behavioral acceptance criteria + success criteria (minted into the ChangeContract from `agreement.md`), with an advisory implementation-free guard.
3. Design stage owns design-derived technical criteria (perf/security/scale budgets) + the plan to satisfy discovery's AC + vision; forbids inventing new user-facing AC; AC invalidated by design route back via routine `adv_change_reenter` from discovery.
4. Prep stage owns task mapping + sequencing + technical-doc readiness; explicitly does not firm criteria.
5. Retarget machine enforcement: relocate `checkMissingSuccessCriteria` from proposal to discovery; rename the proposal criteria heading to `User Outcomes` to avoid the `contract-mint` / clarify-check heading collision.
6. Align specs (`adv-proposal`, `adv-discover`, `adv-design`, `adv-prep`, `advance-workflow`) + the four command files + `docs/adv-gates.md` + manifest + tests; adopt ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148 "implementation-free" and dual-track "Definition of Ready" terminology.

## Success Criteria

- Each pre-execution stage owns one distinct concern with no upstream leakage of AC-grade or design work.
- The proposal stage is materially lighter — no testable-success-criteria obligation, no design decisions.
- The criteria-ownership model reads as established practice (29148 / dual-track), not bespoke ADV invention.
- No regression to the acceptance-gate contract machinery; full build green.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Given a new proposal run, when it completes the proposal gate, then it is not required to produce testable/INVEST success criteria or engineering AC, and a proposal lacking testable success criteria produces no blocking or planning-gate finding on that basis.
2. Given the proposal spec + command, when inspected, then proposal owns a `## User Outcomes` section (implementation-free, non-gating) and explicitly defers engineering AC + success criteria to discovery.
3. Given a discovery run, when agreement is formed, then discovery firms and user-confirms design-independent behavioral acceptance criteria + success criteria, and these mint into the ChangeContract (`AC*`/`SC*`) as today.
4. Given a discovery AC that encodes a mechanism or component, when discovery runs its criteria scan, then ADV emits an advisory finding marking it preliminary/likely design-derived, without hard-blocking the discovery gate.
5. Given the design spec + command, when inspected, then design owns design-derived technical criteria and is prohibited from inventing new user-facing AC, and the design→discovery re-entry path for invalidated AC is documented as routine.
6. Given a change with no proposal-level success-criteria section, when it reaches the planning gate, then no clarify-readiness finding fires on proposal success-criteria, and criteria enforcement is anchored to discovery's agreement/contract.
7. Given the full change, when `pnpm run check` and the test suite run, then both pass, including updated asset tests for the four command files and the affected validator, snapshot, and manifest tests.
8. Given the acceptance gate, when a change is reviewed, then the ChangeContract `AC*` review matrix and evidence policies behave exactly as before this change.

## Constraints

1. Specs-as-laws: all spec deltas authored, validated, and conformant before release.
2. Preserve the crash-safe, artifact-backed gate model.
3. Honor `clarify_enforcement` feature-flag semantics (off / advisory / strict).
4. No regression to ChangeContract `AC*` minting, review matrix, or evidence policies.

## Avoidances

1. Do not conflate proposal `User Outcomes` with engineering acceptance criteria.
2. Do not hard-block the discovery gate on the implementation-free guard (advisory only).
3. Do not redesign the `/adv-task` fast-track in this change (tracked follow-up `ag-JpHqmo1c`).
4. Do not add backward-compatibility migration shims for existing proposals (no special handling).
5. Do not silently change the `clarify_enforcement` default.

## Out of Scope

1. Execution, acceptance, and release gate responsibilities.
2. ChangeContract `AC*` schema or evidence-policy redesign.
3. `/adv-task` fast-track redesign (follow-up `ag-JpHqmo1c`).
4. Backward-compat migration for in-flight changes carrying proposal success criteria.

## Preview Applicability

`visual_surface: false` — this change edits workflow-contract markdown (command files), spec JSON, plugin TypeScript (validator/gate/snapshot/manifest), and docs. No browser-visible or visual output is affected.

## Decisions

### User Decisions

- **Proposal criteria name** → `User Outcomes`. Distinct, implementation-free; avoids the engineering AC/SC heading collision.
- **`checkMissingSuccessCriteria` fate** → relocate to discovery; anchor SC/AC presence enforcement to the agreement/contract.
- **Implementation-free guard** → advisory finding (flag as preliminary/belongs-to-design); no hard block.
- **`/adv-task` fast-track** → out of scope; tracked as follow-up `ag-JpHqmo1c`.
- **Backward-compat** → no special handling; new model applies uniformly (clean because the proposal-level check is removed, not re-pointed at old proposals).

### Agent Decisions (LBP)

- Adopt ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148 "implementation-free" requirement terminology and dual-track "Definition of Ready" framing in spec/command text.
- Frame design→discovery re-entry as routine iterative refinement, not an exceptional path (researcher caution C1).
- Retain the sibling proposal-level clarify checks (`checkUnclearScope`, `checkAssumptionHeavy`, `checkMissingErrorHandling`) — they are context/boundary checks, not AC firming.
- Add an explicit design-derived criteria category for un-firmable budgets (perf/security/scale) per researcher caution C2.

## Deferred Questions

None. Two design-stage open items carry forward as design inputs (not user-deferred): (a) whether proposal `User Outcomes` seed discovery AC or are independent inputs; (b) exact placement/shape of the relocated discovery criteria check.

## Sign-Off

Acceptance criteria approved by user inline ("approve") on the discovery AC checkpoint. Objectives, constraints, avoidances, and decisions confirmed via the discovery open-question round (5 decisions).
documents.design:
# Design

## Architecture Overview

A workflow-contract rebalance applied **law-first** across four layers: specs → command contracts → plugin enforcement code → tests/docs. No new subsystems. The change re-homes one machine check, re-labels one artifact section, and propagates stage-ownership language. The engineering ChangeContract (minted from `agreement.md` at discovery via `contract-mint.ts`) is the existing, authoritative criteria-enforcement point — the rebalance leans on it instead of building new enforcement.

## Key Decisions

1. **Relocate = retire, not re-point.** Discovery's `adv_contract_mint` already requires ≥1 contract item (`CONTRACT_ITEMS_EMPTY` throws otherwise), and the discovery gate is artifact-backed by `agreement.md` plus `DISCOVERY_CONTRACT_MISSING`. The "relocated" enforcement therefore already exists as a structural floor. So `checkMissingSuccessCriteria` is **retired** — removed from `runClarifyReadinessChecks` and deleted with its code path + `CLARIFY_MISSING_SUCCESS_CRITERIA` code + tests — not re-pointed at `agreement.md`. Net enforcement preserved (AC6, AC8). Validator-confirmed: the post-discovery floor is stronger than the removed proposal-text warning.

2. **Proposal `## User Outcomes` heading.** Rename the proposal criteria section `Success Criteria` → `User Outcomes`. Avoids the `contract-mint` `SECTION_MAPPINGS` + clarify-check heading regex collision **by construction**. Proposal B/F/S F-category (adv-proposal.md Phase 2.6) checks for User Outcomes presence (implementation-free), not testable success criteria.

3. **Advisory implementation-free guard.** Discovery's criteria scan emits an *advisory* finding when an AC encodes a mechanism/component (references a specific library/class/algorithm/data structure). Command-text-driven (agent honor-system) with an optional bounded regex assist. Never hard-blocks (per user decision). P33-compliant: the heuristic is advisory-only and human-owned; correctness still rests on the structural contract.

4. **Design-derived criteria category.** `design.md` gains a `## Design-Derived Criteria` section for budgets/limits that fall out of architecture (perf/security/scale). `adv-design` spec + command forbid inventing new user-facing AC; design-invalidated user-facing AC route back via `adv_change_reenter` from discovery, framed as routine iterative refinement (researcher C1).

5. **Spec edits, not append-only.** `adv-proposal` requirements that mandate success criteria (purpose line + rq bodies at spec lines ~5/12/23/43/60/152: `rq-prop-out1`, `rq-prop-scope1/1.2`, `rq-prop-tax2.1`) are **edited** to user-outcomes semantics; new `rq-*` added for the stage-ownership invariants. A `grep` sweep of `success criteria` in the adv-proposal spec gates completeness.

6. **Terminology.** Adopt ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148 "implementation-free" and dual-track "Definition of Ready" framing in spec/command text (researcher recommendation).

## Implementation Strategy / Sequencing

Law-first; TDD on logic-bearing plugin edits.

1. **Spec deltas:** `adv-proposal` (edit success-criteria reqs → User Outcomes; add `rq-stageProposalUserOutcomes01`), `adv-discover` (add `rq-stageDiscoveryFirmAC01` + `rq-stageDiscoveryImplFreeGuard01`), `adv-design` (add `rq-stageDesignDerivedCriteria01`), `advance-workflow` (add `rq-stageEnforcementRetarget01`). Each delta carries ≥1 Given/When/Then. Run `adv_change_validate` + conformance.
2. **Command contracts:** `adv-proposal.md` (Phase 2 section list, Phase 2.6 F-category, Output), `adv-discover.md` (explicit AC+SC firming ownership + advisory implementation-free guard in the criteria scan / AC checkpoint), `adv-design.md` (Design-Derived Criteria section + no-new-user-facing-AC rule + routine re-entry), `adv-prep.md` (clarify prep does not firm criteria).
3. **Plugin code (TDD red→green):** retire `checkMissingSuccessCriteria` + remove from `runClarifyReadinessChecks` (clarify-readiness.ts) and the planning-gate path (gate.ts); remove `CLARIFY_MISSING_SUCCESS_CRITERIA` enum + `tool-formatters.ts:269` message + `types/changes.ts:171` comment ref; repoint `context-snapshot.ts` `countSuccessCriteria` to count agreement AC (display-only) or drop; update `manifest.ts:99` proposal description string; update scaffold-template emitters `storage/json.ts:591/661` + `tools/change.ts:166-172` (`## Success Criteria` → `## User Outcomes`).
4. **Tests:** RED→GREEN for plugin changes; update asset tests for the four command files; update `clarify-readiness.test.ts`, `clarify-readiness.e2e.test.ts`, `status.test.ts`, `context-snapshot.test.ts`.
5. **Docs:** `docs/adv-gates.md` Proposal/Discovery/Design gate-behavior rows.
6. **Gate:** full `pnpm run check` + suite green.

## Affected Components

Primary seam: `gate.ts` `runClarifyReadinessChecks` ← `clarify-readiness.ts` (retire one check). `contract-mint.ts` is unaffected (parses `input.agreement`, never `proposal.md`) — verified, no acceptance-gate regression. Additional spec touched: `chat-output-display/spec.json` (MUST on "success criteria count" tied to `context-snapshot.countSuccessCriteria` — repoint/drop). Full file list per proposal Affected Code + the scaffold-template emitters above.

## Design-Derived Criteria

- The advisory implementation-free heuristic MUST be bounded (keyword/regex assist), false-positive-tolerant (advisory only), and MUST NOT gate any transition.
- Removing a `ClarifyReadinessCode` MUST keep the remaining codes stable (no behavioral renumbering of other checks).
- The `context-snapshot` display change MUST update snapshot-format fixtures so existing format tests stay green.

## LBP Analysis

Law-first sequencing plus leaning on the existing `contract-mint` enforcement is long-term correct: it **reduces** enforcement surface rather than adding a parallel check. Validated against 29148 / dual-track agile (adv-researcher PASS). Rejected alternative: re-pointing `checkMissingSuccessCriteria` at `agreement.md` — duplicates the contract-mint guarantee for zero added assurance, more code + tests.

## Risks / Mitigations

- **R:** removing `CLARIFY_MISSING_SUCCESS_CRITERIA` breaks status/e2e tests. **M:** update those tests in-change (in scope).
- **R:** strict-mode projects perceive lost enforcement. **M:** AC8 regression check + document discovery contract mint as the enforcement point.
- **R:** an edited-not-appended adv-proposal spec misses a requirement. **M:** `grep` sweep of `success criteria` in the spec + conformance gate.
- **R:** advisory guard false positives annoy. **M:** advisory-only, no gate block; tune keyword set.

## Validator Result & Prep Refinements

**Independent validator (adv-researcher, Phase 3.5): VALIDATED.** Verified against real code: removing `checkMissingSuccessCriteria` creates no enforcement gap — discovery is structurally double-gated by the `agreement.md` artifact-backed check (`gate-readiness.ts`) and `DISCOVERY_CONTRACT_MISSING`; contract mint requires ≥1 item (`contract-mint.ts:203-207`). Rename claim confirmed: `SECTION_MAPPINGS` parses `input.agreement` only. No viable alternative overlooked.

Non-blocking refinements folded into prep (validator agenda: `ag-HHWF51De`, `ag-xYOw5fHJ`, `ag-P7k57DXl`, `ag-JOcYsX4o`):

1. **Wording precision:** spec/command text describing the discovery enforcement floor should say "≥1 contract item present", not "AC/SC present" — `CONTRACT_ITEMS_EMPTY` accepts any of `SC/AC/C/DONT/OOS`. A change with only Constraints/OOS mints + passes acceptance with zero AC; this is **pre-existing** behavior preserved by AC8, not a regression introduced here.
2. **Rename edit list expansion:** include scaffold-template emitters `storage/json.ts:591/661` and `tools/change.ts:166-172` (they WRITE `## Success Criteria`), plus the `chat-output-display` spec dependency on `countSuccessCriteria`.
3. **Bounded removal verification:** confirm the `CLARIFY_MISSING_SUCCESS_CRITERIA` removal set is exactly `clarify-readiness.ts` + its test + `tool-formatters.ts:269` + `types/changes.ts:171` comment (archive `change.json` hits are frozen records, not consumers).
documents.executiveSummary:
# Executive Summary

## Outcome

ADV workflow-stage boundaries were sharpened so proposal now owns implementation-free User Outcomes, discovery owns confirmed behavioral AC/SC, design owns design-derived technical criteria, and prep maps approved criteria into tasks without rewriting them.

## Verdict

APPROVED

## What Was Built

1. Proposal spec/command/scaffold/snapshot language moved from testable Success Criteria to implementation-free User Outcomes.
2. Discovery/design/prep specs and command contracts now define their stage-owned criteria boundaries, including advisory discovery implementation-free findings and routine design→discovery re-entry.
3. Plugin enforcement retired the proposal-level `checkMissingSuccessCriteria` / `CLARIFY_MISSING_SUCCESS_CRITERIA` path while preserving discovery ChangeContract enforcement.
4. Manifest, README, ADV instructions, generated spec docs, and asset tests were aligned to the new boundary model.
5. Acceptance-review remediation fixed stale stage-boundary wording across manifest/docs/spec mirrors.

## What Was Verified

- Verdict: APPROVED with 0 blockers and 0 issues after acceptance review remediation.
- Tests: targeted validator/manifest/snapshot/storage/command-asset suites passed; `bin/oc-test smoke` passed; `bin/oc-test full` passed.
- Preview URL: not_applicable — agreement declares `visual_surface: false`; implementation changed workflow specs, commands, docs, and plugin TypeScript only; no browser-visible surface delivered.
- Contract matrix: 25/25 rows passed, respected, or not_applicable; 0 failed, violated, unknown, or missing rows.
- Validation: `adv_change_validate strict` passed with one accepted `NO_DELTAS` warning because spec-law edits were applied directly in repo specs.

## Remaining Concerns

None.
documents.acceptance:
# Acceptance

Reviewed at: 2026-06-05T23:23:30.000Z

## Contract Review Matrix

| ID | Kind | Requirement | Status | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| SC1 | success_criterion | Each pre-execution stage owns one distinct concern with no upstream leakage of AC-grade or design work. | pass | Review READY; specs/commands/docs now separate Proposal=User Outcomes, Discovery=AC/SC, Design=design-derived criteria, Prep=task mapping. Stale wording scan passed. |
| SC2 | success_criterion | The proposal stage is materially lighter — no testable-success-criteria obligation, no design decisions. | pass | Proposal spec/command/storage/snapshot changed from Success Criteria to User Outcomes; clarify readiness no longer blocks missing proposal success criteria. |
| SC3 | success_criterion | The criteria-ownership model reads as established practice (29148 / dual-track), not bespoke ADV invention. | pass | Agreement/design adopted ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148 implementation-free wording and dual-track Definition-of-Ready framing; adv-researcher validation PASS. |
| SC4 | success_criterion | No regression to the acceptance-gate contract machinery; full build green. | pass | bin/oc-test smoke passed; bin/oc-test full passed; acceptance review READY; contract review matrix persisted through typed tool. |
| AC1 | acceptance_criterion | Given a new proposal run, when it completes the proposal gate, then it is not required to produce testable/INVEST success criteria or engineering AC, and a proposal lacking testable success criteria produces no blocking or planning-gate finding on that basis. | pass | clarify-readiness tests updated: proposal without testable success criteria no longer emits CLARIFY_MISSING_SUCCESS_CRITERIA; targeted validator tests passed. |
| AC2 | acceptance_criterion | Given the proposal spec + command, when inspected, then proposal owns a `## User Outcomes` section (implementation-free, non-gating) and explicitly defers engineering AC + success criteria to discovery. | pass | adv-proposal spec/command/scaffold/snapshot tests pass; proposal owns implementation-free User Outcomes and defers engineering AC/SC to discovery. |
| AC3 | acceptance_criterion | Given a discovery run, when agreement is formed, then discovery firms and user-confirms design-independent behavioral acceptance criteria + success criteria, and these mint into the ChangeContract (`AC*`/`SC*`) as today. | pass | Discovery agreement still minted 25 ChangeContract items (SC*, AC*, C*, DONT*, OOS*) from agreement.md; contract present in adv_change_show. |
| AC4 | acceptance_criterion | Given a discovery AC that encodes a mechanism or component, when discovery runs its criteria scan, then ADV emits an advisory finding marking it preliminary/likely design-derived, without hard-blocking the discovery gate. | pass | adv-discover spec/command updated for advisory implementation-free criteria scan; asset tests pass; no hard-blocking gate path added. |
| AC5 | acceptance_criterion | Given the design spec + command, when inspected, then design owns design-derived technical criteria and is prohibited from inventing new user-facing AC, and the design→discovery re-entry path for invalidated AC is documented as routine. | pass | adv-design spec/command updated for Design-Derived Criteria, no-new-user-facing-AC rule, and routine adv_change_reenter from discovery; asset tests pass. |
| AC6 | acceptance_criterion | Given a change with no proposal-level success-criteria section, when it reaches the planning gate, then no clarify-readiness finding fires on proposal success-criteria, and criteria enforcement is anchored to discovery's agreement/contract. | pass | checkMissingSuccessCriteria and CLARIFY_MISSING_SUCCESS_CRITERIA removed from plugin/src; planning-gate criteria enforcement anchored to agreement/contract; validator tests pass. |
| AC7 | acceptance_criterion | Given the full change, when `pnpm run check` and the test suite run, then both pass, including updated asset tests for the four command files and the affected validator, snapshot, and manifest tests. | pass | bin/oc-test smoke passed; bin/oc-test full passed; targeted affected tests passed (manifest, snapshot, storage, clarify-readiness, command assets). |
| AC8 | acceptance_criterion | Given the acceptance gate, when a change is reviewed, then the ChangeContract `AC*` review matrix and evidence policies behave exactly as before this change. | pass | Review matrix rows set for all 25 contract items; preview URL not_applicable because agreement visual_surface:false and no visual-output files/tasks were implemented. |
| C1 | constraint | Specs-as-laws: all spec deltas authored, validated, and conformant before release. | respected | adv_change_validate strict passed; NO_DELTAS warning accepted because spec-law edits were applied directly in repo specs; spec mirrors/docs updated. |
| C2 | constraint | Preserve the crash-safe, artifact-backed gate model. | respected | All tasks completed via ADV task checkpoints; gates advanced through ADV tools; worktree isolation used for mutations. |
| C3 | constraint | Honor `clarify_enforcement` feature-flag semantics (off / advisory / strict). | respected | No clarify_enforcement default change made; retirement limited to proposal success-criteria check; sibling clarify checks remain. |
| C4 | constraint | No regression to ChangeContract `AC*` minting, review matrix, or evidence policies. | respected | ChangeContract schema/evidence policies untouched; AC*/SC* minting still present; typed review matrix set through adv_contract_review_matrix_set. |
| DONT1 | avoidance | Do not conflate proposal `User Outcomes` with engineering acceptance criteria. | respected | Proposal terminology changed to User Outcomes; discovery remains AC/SC owner; docs and manifest aligned. |
| DONT2 | avoidance | Do not hard-block the discovery gate on the implementation-free guard (advisory only). | respected | Discovery implementation-free guard documented as advisory; no hard-blocking code path introduced. |
| DONT3 | avoidance | Do not redesign the `/adv-task` fast-track in this change (tracked follow-up `ag-JpHqmo1c`). | respected | /adv-task fast-track redesign not implemented; follow-up ag-JpHqmo1c retained. |
| DONT4 | avoidance | Do not add backward-compatibility migration shims for existing proposals (no special handling). | respected | No backward-compat migration shim added; new model applies uniformly by removing the proposal-level check. |
| DONT5 | avoidance | Do not silently change the `clarify_enforcement` default. | respected | No code/config change to clarify_enforcement default found in touched files or review evidence. |
| OOS1 | out_of_scope | Execution, acceptance, and release gate responsibilities. | not_applicable | This change did not alter execution, acceptance, or release gate responsibilities beyond review-time evidence artifacts required by the workflow. |
| OOS2 | out_of_scope | ChangeContract `AC*` schema or evidence-policy redesign. | not_applicable | No ChangeContract AC* schema or evidence-policy redesign performed; existing contract machinery used. |
| OOS3 | out_of_scope | `/adv-task` fast-track redesign (follow-up `ag-JpHqmo1c`). | not_applicable | /adv-task fast-track redesign excluded; follow-up ag-JpHqmo1c tracks it. |
| OOS4 | out_of_scope | Backward-compat migration for in-flight changes carrying proposal success criteria. | not_applicable | No migration for in-flight changes carrying proposal success criteria was added. |
